title: Meridian Private Credit Opportunities
content:
MERIDIAN PRIVATE CREDIT OPPORTUNITIES - PRODUCT FACTSHEET

OVERVIEW
Meridian Private Credit Opportunities (MPCO) is a direct lending strategy targeting middle-market companies with EBITDA between $10-100 million. The fund provides senior secured loans, unitranche facilities, and mezzanine financing to sponsor-backed and non-sponsor transactions. This offering is structured as a private fund under SEC Regulation D, Rule 506(c), limiting participation to accredited investors only.

INVESTMENT STRATEGY
The fund focuses on floating-rate senior secured loans with typical spreads of L+450-750 basis points over SOFR. Portfolio companies are primarily located in defensive sectors including healthcare services, business services, and essential infrastructure. Target loan sizes range from $25-150 million per transaction, with the fund maintaining a diversified approach across 40-60 portfolio companies at full deployment.

Portfolio Manager Robert J. Williams, CFA, leads the investment committee with over 18 years of private credit experience. The team has originated approximately $2.8 billion in direct lending transactions since inception, maintaining a net loss rate below 1.2% across market cycles.

RISK CONSIDERATIONS
This investment carries significant risks appropriate only for sophisticated investors. Credit risk remains the primary concern, as borrowers may default on obligations during economic downturns. Interest rate sensitivity, while mitigated by floating-rate structures, can impact refinancing activities and portfolio company valuations. Liquidity risk is substantial - investors should expect limited redemption opportunities with quarterly windows subject to 2% NAV caps and potential gate provisions.

Illiquidity premiums are inherent to this asset class, and investors may be unable to access capital for extended periods. Market volatility can significantly impact portfolio valuations, particularly during credit cycle contractions. Concentration risk exists despite diversification efforts, as middle-market lending represents a specialized sector vulnerable to economic disruptions.

REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
All recommendations must comply with FINRA Rule 2111 suitability requirements. Advisors must conduct thorough due diligence regarding client investment experience, financial situation, and risk tolerance before presenting MPCO. This product is suitable only for clients with substantial investable assets exceeding $2 million and demonstrated alternative investment experience.

Minimum investment thresholds are $500,000 for new investors, with subsequent contributions of $100,000 minimum. Qualified clients under Investment Advisers Act Section 205(e) may access performance-based fee arrangements. Account #4821-7734 serves as the master account for all subscription processing and capital calls.

PERFORMANCE METRICS
Since inception in January 2019, the fund has generated net IRR of 11.8% through December 2024, with current yield of approximately 9.2%. Default rates have remained below industry averages at 0.8% by count and 1.1% by dollar value. Portfolio duration averages 4.2 years with 89% of loans carrying first-lien security positions.

Management fees are structured at 1.75% annually on committed capital during the investment period, transitioning to invested capital thereafter. Performance fees of 15% apply above an 8% preferred return with full catch-up provisions.

For additional information regarding suitability assessments or subscription procedures, contact our Private Wealth team at (212) 555-0147. All marketing materials require pre-approval through Meridian's compliance department prior to client distribution.

This document contains confidential information intended solely for Senior-level advisors and above. Distribution outside authorized personnel requires written approval from Legal & Compliance.
